scenario: Single 34,000-gal tank of methyl methacrylate (MMA) at GKN Aerospace, 12122 Western Ave, Garden Grove CA; ~7,000 gal currently. MW 100; liquid density ~0.94 kg/L; net heat of combustion ~26.4 MJ/kg; vapor ~3.4x denser than air. IDLH 1000 ppm, ERPG-2 75 ppm, ERPG-1 10 ppm. Conditions: wind 8 mph (3.6 m/s), Pasquill D, urban.
units: blast TNT in metric tonnes; all radii/distances in metres
plume_driver: Plume reach is driven by the SOURCE/LEAK RATE (kg/s), not tank volume. All distances below are anchored to ~4 kg/s pool evaporation at 8 mph; the simulator's leak-rate slider scales them (distance ~ Q^0.5). Leak rate is PER TANK: a cascade of N failing tanks uses an effective source of leak*N, so both the blast (combined fuel) and the plume (combined release) grow with the cascade. The interactive selector exposes two dense-gas models (Britter-McQuaid, DEGADIS); the neutral-Gaussian numbers are retained here as a lower-bound 'what if it were a passive gas' reference but were removed from the public UI to avoid being misread as the safe distance.

prompts.blast: First-principles VCE: fuel mass; justified VCE yield; TNT-equivalent (TNT 4.6 MJ/kg) tonnes; Hopkinson-Cranz overpressure radii (fireball/total destruction, severe ~10 psi, moderate ~3 psi, glass-break ~1 psi).
prompts.plume_densegas: First-principles dense-gas dispersion: max downwind distance to IDLH/ERPG-2/ERPG-1; state method + release-rate/flash assumptions.
prompts.plume_gaussian: Neutral-buoyancy GAUSSIAN plume (passive gas, ignore dense-gas slumping) at ~4 kg/s continuous release; max downwind distance to IDLH/ERPG-2/ERPG-1.
prompts.plume_degadis: DEGADIS two-phase dense-gas model: gravity-driven slumping phase (vertical mixing suppressed) governed by the bulk Richardson number Ri_b = g'*L_v/u*^2; hand off to a passive Gaussian phase with a virtual source matched to the flattened cloud once Ri_b < ~0.1. Report max downwind distance to IDLH/ERPG-2/ERPG-1, the transition distance, and the assumed evaporative source term (kg/s).

claude.note: Claude is the interactive default map; its Gaussian = the calibrated calcPlumeGaussian consensus; DEGADIS = calcPlumeDEGADIS anchor.

notes: Screening-level (dense-gas ±factor 2-3). TNT-equivalent is blast energy only (not radiation/fallout). Across the three dispersion models, the spread is driven mostly by each model's evaporation source-term, not the dispersion math. DEGADIS is two-phase: gravity slumping until the bulk Richardson number Ri_b falls to ~0.1, then a passive Gaussian phase from a virtual source; it lands between the B-M dense-gas and neutral-Gaussian footprints. The neutral-Gaussian column is a passive-gas lower bound (understates a dense gas like MMA); it is retained here for reference but not exposed in the UI.
